text: When setting project goals, it’s important to make them SMART: Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. This framework helps ensure your goals are clear and realistic. Once you have your main goals, break them down into smaller milestones. These are key points along the way that help you track progress. You should also define your deliverables, the specific outputs or products you’ll create, like a report or a prototype. Finally, stakeholder alignment is crucial. Everyone involved, from your team to clients, should understand and agree on the goals and milestones. This prevents confusion and keeps the project moving smoothly.
